summary.intent: Fix a PR review comment that identified a missing 'profile' path in a warning message within the config validation logic.
summary.outcome: The warning message in validate.rs was updated to include 'profile' as a valid suppression path, all 76 validate tests passed, and the fix was committed and pushed to the open PR #661.

repo: Suppression logic in crates/config/src/validate.rs checks profile.is_none() but the warning message previously omitted 'profile' as a valid suppression path — a mismatch between logic and user-facing messaging.
The project uses 'just lint', 'just test', and 'local-validate.sh' for workspace-wide validation gates before merge.
PR template includes Summary, Validation (Completed/Remaining), and Manual QA sections.

code.finding: Warning message for suppression paths was missing 'profile' as a valid option, even though the suppression logic correctly checks profile.is_none(). Existing tests only assert on preset names and '[tools.policy]' substring, so this discrepancy was not caught by tests.

workflow: Greptile flagged a P2 code review comment about message/logic mismatch that human reviewers missed.
YubiKey tap is required per global git config for push operations.
Per-crate test gates can be green while workspace-wide sweep is still pending — these are tracked separately as a merge prerequisite.

friction: Workspace-wide lint/test sweep (just lint, just test, local-validate.sh) cannot be run until a PR number is assigned, creating a deferred validation step.
open_items: Full workspace-wide 'just lint' / 'just test' / 'local-validate.sh' sweep still needs to be run against PR #661 before merge.
Manual QA section in the PR description is listed as remaining work.
